notation.name: complex channel sample
notation.meaning: Complex sample at channel index c and sample index n in the external ordered capture model.

notation.name: quaternion lane sample
notation.meaning: Quaternion-valued sample in grouped lane g at sample index n after canonical lift.

notation.name: single quaternion sample
notation.meaning: Quaternion with coordinates q0 + q1 i + q2 j + q3 k used for pair-level lift and proofs.

notation.name: quaternion algebra
notation.meaning: Hamilton quaternion algebra used as the internal lane value space.

notation.name: channel count
notation.meaning: Number of ordered complex input channels in the capture.

notation.name: samples per channel
notation.meaning: Number of temporal samples in each channel.

notation.name: quaternion lane count
notation.meaning: Number of grouped quaternion lanes produced by the canonical pairing rule.

notation.name: canonical grouped lift
notation.meaning: Forward map from ordered complex-channel captures to grouped quaternion lanes.

notation.name: inverse unpacking map
notation.meaning: Inverse map that reconstructs the ordered complex channels from lifted quaternion lanes.

notation.name: quaternion basis units
notation.meaning: Quaternion imaginary basis units used to encode four real coordinates per lane.
